findings.title: Need for Notification Priority Levels
findings.description: Users require a system to distinguish between critical alerts and routine updates. The current uniformity in notification presentation leads to important messages being overlooked.
assumptions: Users are overwhelmed by the current notification system.

evidence.excerpt: Users need a way to distinguish between critical alerts and routine updates.
Her biggest frustration is that transactional alerts carry the same visual weight as critical incidents.
She would pay more for a tier that offers intelligent filtering.

recommendations: Implement a three-tier priority system for notifications.

findings.title: Desire for Notification Digest Feature
findings.description: Casual users prefer receiving non-urgent notifications in a digest format rather than as individual alerts, which would help reduce notification fatigue.
assumptions: Users want to manage notification volume effectively.

evidence.excerpt: He wants digest-style summaries instead of individual pings for non-urgent updates.

recommendations: Develop a feature for batching low-priority notifications into digest formats.

findings.title: Granular Notification Preferences Needed
findings.description: Admins require the ability to set granular notification preferences for teams and individuals, allowing for better customization and control over notification types.
assumptions: Organizations need tailored notification settings to improve user experience.

evidence.excerpt: Users should be able to opt in/out of specific notification categories.
Her top request is granular notification preferences.

recommendations: Create a user preference page for configuring notification categories.

findings.title: Accessibility Concerns with Notification Center
findings.description: There are significant accessibility issues within the notification center, particularly for users with visual impairments, highlighting the need for inclusive design practices.
assumptions: Accessibility is a critical factor for user satisfaction.

evidence.excerpt: Several team members using screen readers have difficulty navigating the notification center.
The color-only priority indicators are problematic for colorblind users.

recommendations: Conduct an accessibility audit and redesign the notification center.

summary: The analysis reveals critical insights into user needs for SmartNotify, particularly around notification prioritization, digest features, granular preferences, and accessibility. These findings are supported by multiple sources, indicating a clear direction for product enhancements to improve user experience and satisfaction.
